findings.problem: Continuity break: the safe is opened with a different combination than the one established at the end of chapter 14, creating a canonical contradiction in a causally critical handoff beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:27, reconcile the dial sequence with the established prior-chapter combination. If the number change is intentional, add explicit on-page explanation between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:27 before the safe opens.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised text at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:27 either matches the established combination or explicitly explains why a different sequence is required before the safe opens; FAIL if the mismatch remains unexplained.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift via summary: a two-hour, high-risk evidence capture is compressed into a single retrospective paragraph, replacing lived scene pressure with procedural recap at the precise point where legal and personal stakes should stay embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace or expand snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:79 with an on-page micro-sequence that includes one concrete interruption risk, one chain-of-custody vulnerability, and one immediate bodily stress marker from Lena before returning to forward motion at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:81.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:79 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:81 contains a dramatized moment where chain integrity is actively threatened and recovered in-scene; FAIL if it remains a blanket summary of work completed.

findings.problem: Decision causality is partially duplicated: Lena appears to decide full unredacted release at line 125, then the chapter replays the same decisive beat at line 235 without a fully explicit procedural gate, softening climax efficiency.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten causal clarity by making the provisional-vs-final distinction explicit at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:125-133, then escalate new stakes (not just repeated stakes) before the final authorization at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:231-236.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a first-time reader can point to explicit text in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:125-133 that makes line 125 provisional and line 235 final under materially changed pressure; FAIL if both beats still read as the same decision repeated.

findings.problem: Voice separation compresses under pressure: Mara and Corinne converge into similar legal-brief diction, weakening idiolect contrast. Mara's profile calls for high-contraction, evidence-verb skepticism, but several lines drift toward counsel-speak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Mara's lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:121, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:211 toward faster, more colloquial, attribution-driven phrasing; preserve Corinne's formal legal precision at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207 as contrast.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if readers can correctly attribute Mara vs Corinne in at least four of five consecutive turns in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:199-212 without tags, and Mara uses contractions in at least three of her four turns; FAIL if both voices still sound legally interchangeable.

findings.problem: Mechanical repetition: the construction "The [ordinal] stack held ..." appears three times in a row, flattening rhythm and signaling list autopilot during a key discovery sequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:41-46 so only one sentence (max) uses the current inventory template; vary syntax and integrate Lena's immediate internal/sensory response into at least one of the document reveals.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:41-46 contains no more than one "The ... stack held" opener and includes at least one sentence linking a specific document to Lena's immediate internal or bodily reaction; FAIL if the three-step catalogue formula remains.

summary: Chapter 15 lands major consequence and maintains close-third pressure, but it fails review due one HIGH continuity contradiction (safe combination mismatch), one HIGH sanitization-by-summary compression at a critical risk window, and medium-level dialogue/prose craft drag from duplicated decision framing, idiolect convergence, and repeated inventory sentence formulas.
